topic: Modules - Inline Modules and Test Module Conventions

turns.content: I've read about inline mod tests blocks before, but I still don't fully understand why #[cfg(test)] is required on them or why the test module is private by default. My tool is trying to automate a repetitive task and I need to separate unit tests from integration tests properly.

Think of your binary crate like a bakery where the main code is the flour and ingredients, while the test module is the recipe book kept in a locked cabinet. The #[cfg(test)] attribute is simply the key that only unlocks the cabinet when you run tests with cargo test; without it, the cabinet stays closed for normal builds. The test module is private by default because unit tests should stay isolated from your public API so they don't accidentally force users to depend on internal implementation details. When separating concerns, keep inline mod tests inside lib.rs or src/lib.rs for quick unit checks, while integration tests go in a separate directory like tests/ where you can import the crate under test and verify end-to-end behavior without needing #[cfg(test)] on every single module.
